description.en: `/etc/shells` is a text file which contains the full pathnames of valid login shells. This file is consulted by `chsh` and available to be queried by other programs.

rationale.en: It is critical to ensure that the `/etc/shells` file is protected from unauthorized access. Although it is protected by default, the file permissions could be changed either inadvertently or through malicious actions.

audit.en:
Run the following commands to remove excess permissions, set owner, and set group on `/etc/shells`:

```
# chmod u-x,go-wx /etc/shells
# chown root:root /etc/shells
```

remediation.en:
Run the following commands to remove excess permissions, set owner, and set group on `/etc/shells`:

```
# chmod u-x,go-wx /etc/shells
# chown root:root /etc/shells
```
